revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神社覈録]]
[[File:Jinja Kakuroku hombun cropped Jinja Kakuroku Part Jo Frame 8.jpg|thumb|250px|Text of the Western‐style compiled book published by [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Kōtentenkokkyūsho]]<ref>{{ill|Suzuka Tsuratane|de|Suzuka Tsuratane|ja|鈴鹿連胤|lt=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|lt_ja=中臣朝臣連胤|lt_de=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tsuratane|1=Suzuka Tsuratane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536446}} compiled “Hyōmoku”, a work by Suzuka Yoshikai; edited by {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|lt=Inoue Yorimitsu|lt_ja=井上賴囶|lt_de=Inoue Yorimitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535302}} and {{ill|Saeki Yugi|de|Saeki Yugi|ja|佐伯有義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Yugi|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382847}} in the upper volume of 『Jinja Kakuroku』, [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Kōtentenkokkyūsho]], 1902, page 1.</ref>]] 『'''Jinja Kakuroku''' (神社覈録)』 is a work that examines ancient shrines, beginning with the {{ill|System Shrine|de|System Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/System Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It was authored by {{ill|Suzuka Tsuratane|de|Suzuka Tsuratane|ja|鈴鹿連胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tsuratane|1=Suzuka Tsuratane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536446}} (Suzuka Tsuratane) and comprises 75 volumes in total. It was initially drafted in the 7th year of {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tempō|1=Tenpō}} (1836) and was completed in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Meiji era|1=Emperor Meiji}} (1870). == Overview == [[File:Jinja Kakuroku cropped Jinja Kakuroku Part Jo Frame 429.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Chapter on “Tōtō Province” of the Western‐style compiled book published by [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Kōtentenkokkyūsho]]<ref>{{ill|Suzuka Tsuratane|de|Suzuka Tsuratane|ja|鈴鹿連胤|lt=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|lt_ja=中臣朝臣連胤|lt_de=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tsuratane|1=Suzuka Tsuratane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536446}} compiled “Tōtō Province”, a work by Suzuka Yoshikai; edited by {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|lt=Inoue Yorimitsu|lt_ja=井上賴囶|lt_de=Inoue Yorimitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535302}} and {{ill|Saeki Yugi|de|Saeki Yugi|ja|佐伯有義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Yugi|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382847}} in the upper volume of 『Jinja Kakuroku』, [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Kōtentenkokkyūsho]], 1902, page 836.</ref>]] The structure of the work is based on examining the readings of shrine names, the enshrined deities, and the locations of shrines by quoting descriptions related to each shrine from various texts—including those on the {{ill|System Shrine|de|System Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/System Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Historically Documented Shrine|de|Historically Documented Shrine|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historically Documented Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|qid=Q10444029}}, and other famous shrines—from sources such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, among others. However, due to the conditions of the time, some of the cited texts were later regarded as {{ill|Forged Text|de|Forged Text|ja|偽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Forged Text|1=forged document|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q42757690}}, which poses certain issues. Moreover, although some sections were intentionally left blank for later completion, it was evaluated as the most comprehensive among similar works, as it referenced the complete text of the known "{{ill|Domestic Divine Names Register|de|Domestic Divine Names Register|ja|国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Domestic Divine Names Register|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420490}}". == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, an imperial command was issued by the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}}, prompting revisions to the draft manuscript and the creation of both a clean copy and a duplicate; the former was presented on November 22, Meiji 3 (1870) (currently housed in the collection of the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|qid=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency's Mausoleum Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}). It is noteworthy that Suzuka Tsuratane himself passed away two days before the presentation. In Meiji 35 (1902), {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|lt=Inoue Yorimitsu|lt_ja=井上賴囶|lt_de=Inoue Yorimitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535302}}—then a lecturer at [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Kōtentenkokkyūsho]]—and {{ill|Saeki Yugi|de|Saeki Yugi|ja|佐伯有義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Yugi|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382847}} used a proof copy from the Suzuka family, prepared by {{ill|Yano Gendō|de|Yano Gendō|ja|矢野玄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Gendō|1=Yano Harumichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6538145}}, as the base text to which they added punctuation and reading marks, and published it as a two‐volume Western‐style compiled book from the same institute. == Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}} == References == * ''Jinja Kakuroku (Reprint Edition)'', {{ill|Shibunkaku Publishing|de|Shibunkaku Publishing|ja|思文閣出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibunkaku Publishing|1=Shibunkaku Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11491528}}, 1971, ISBN 4-901339-06-0 (first edition by [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Kōtentenkokkyūsho]], 1902) * ''Shinto Grand Dictionary (Condensed Edition)'', {{ill|Rinkawa Shoten|de|Rinkawa Shoten|ja|臨川書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkawa Shoten|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612454}}, 1969, ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (first edition by {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}, 1939) * ''Shinto History Grand Dictionary'', {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|de|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}, 2004, ISBN 4-642-01340-7 {{Shinto stub}}
{{translated page|ja|神社覈録|version=103448248|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{List of Shikinaisha}}
[[ja:神社覈録]] [[de:Jinja Kakuroku]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== [[File:Jinja Kakuroku hombun cropped Jinja Kakuroku Part Jo Frame 8.jpg|thumb|250px|[[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Imperial Research Institute]] published the text of the Western-bound book<ref>{{ill|Suzuka Tsuratane|de|Suzuka Tsuratane|ja|鈴鹿連胤|lt=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|lt_ja=中臣朝臣連胤|lt_de=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tsuratane|1=Suzuka Tsuratane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536446}} compiled “Hyōmoku” from the work of Suzuka Yoshi, edited by {{ill|Inoue Yorikata|de|Inoue Yorikata|ja|井上頼圀|lt=Inoue Yori|lt_ja=井上賴囶|lt_de=Inoue Yorikata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorikata|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535302}} and {{ill|Saeki Yūgi|de|Saeki Yūgi|ja|佐伯有義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Yūgi|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382847}}, in the upper volume of ‘{{ill|Jinja Kakuroku|de|Jinja Kakuroku|ja|神社覈録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja Kakuroku|qq=}}’, [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Imperial Research Institute]], 1902, p. 1.</ref>]] '''Jinja Kakuroku''' (神社覈録) is a work investigating ancient shrines – beginning with {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} – authored by {{ill|Suzuka Tsuratane|de|Suzuka Tsuratane|ja|鈴鹿連胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tsuratane|1=Suzuka Tsuratane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536446}} (reading: すずか つらたね) in 75 volumes. It was drafted in the 7th year of {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} ({{ill|1836|en|1836|qid=Q7601|lt=1836|1=1836}}) and completed in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Meiji era|1=Emperor Meiji}} ({{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}).
== Overview == [[File:Jinja Kakuroku cropped Jinja Kakuroku Part Jo Frame 429.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Imperial Research Institute]] published the chapter on “{{ill|Tōtō Province|de|Tōtō-Provinz|ja|遠江國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtō Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遠江国|1=Tōtōmi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1048864}}” in the Western-bound book<ref>{{ill|Suzuka Tsuratane|de|Suzuka Tsuratane|ja|鈴鹿連胤|lt=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|lt_ja=中臣朝臣連胤|lt_de=Nakatomi Asomi Tsuratane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tsuratane|1=Suzuka Tsuratane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536446}} compiled “Tōtō Province” from the work of Suzuka Yoshi, edited by {{ill|Inoue Yorikata|de|Inoue Yorikata|ja|井上頼圀|lt=Inoue Yori|lt_ja=井上賴囶|lt_de=Inoue Yorikata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorikata|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535302}} and {{ill|Saeki Yūgi|de|Saeki Yūgi|ja|佐伯有義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Yūgi|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382847}}, in the upper volume of ‘{{ill|Jinja Kakuroku|de|Jinja Kakuroku|ja|神社覈録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja Kakuroku|qq=}}’, [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Imperial Research Institute]], 1902, p. 836.</ref>]] The work is arranged by quoting passages related to each shrine from various texts – beginning with {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Rokkoku-shi|1=Rikkokushi}} – to examine the readings of shrine names, the enshrined deities, and the locations of the shrines for ancient institutions such as {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Kokushi Mizaisha|de|Kokushi Mizaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mizaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|qid=Q10444029}}, and other notable shrines. However, due to the circumstances of the time, some of the quoted texts include works later regarded as {{ill|spurious works|de|fälschliche Schriften|ja|偽書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spurious works|1=forged document|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q42757690}}. In addition, although certain sections were intentionally left blank for future completion, the work was acclaimed as the most comprehensive among similar texts – for example, by including the full text of the {{ill|Domestic Shrine Name Register|de|Domestic Shrine Name Register|ja|国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Domestic Shrine Name Register|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420490}} as then known.
== History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, an imperial order for presentation was issued from the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}}, prompting a supplemented revision of the draft manuscript into both a clean copy and a transcript. The clean copy was presented on November 22, Meiji 3 ({{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}) (currently held by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|qid=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency Shoryo-bu|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}). Note that Tsuratane himself passed away two days prior to the presentation.
In Meiji 35 ({{ill|1902|en|1902|qid=Q2043|lt=1902|1=1902}}), {{ill|Inoue Yorikata|de|Inoue Yorikata|ja|井上頼圀|lt=Inoue Yori|lt_ja=井上賴囶|lt_de=Inoue Yorikata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorikata|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535302}}, then a lecturer at the [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Imperial Research Institute]], and {{ill|Saeki Yūgi|de|Saeki Yūgi|ja|佐伯有義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Yūgi|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382847}} produced a two-volume Western-bound edition. They based this on a proof copy corrected by {{ill|Yano Gendō|de|Yano Gendō|ja|矢野玄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Gendō|1=Yano Harumichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6538145}}, held by the Suzuka family, adding punctuation and reading marks.
== Notes == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * “Jinja Kakuroku (Reprint Edition)”, published by {{ill|Shibunkaku Publishing|de|Shibunkaku Publishing|ja|思文閣出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibunkaku Publishing|1=Shibunkaku Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11491528}}, 1971, ISBN 4-901339-06-0 (the first edition was by [[:en:Koten Kokyusho|Imperial Research Institute]], 1902) * “Shinto Great Dictionary (Abridged Edition)”, published by {{ill|Rinkyosha|de|Rinkyosha|ja|臨川書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinkyosha|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612454}}, 1969, ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (the first edition was by {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}, 1939) * “Shinto History Great Dictionary”, published by {{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|de|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}, 2004, ISBN 4-642-01340-7
{{shinto-stub}}
{{Shinto navigation}} {{List of Shikinaisha}}
{{translated page|ja|神社覈録|version=103448248|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:神社覈録]] [[de:Records of Shinto Shrines]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Jinja Kakuroku}} {{wikidata link|Q11590708}} [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Jinja Kakuroku]][[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
